mcarper

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: gui working on uconsole + UD100 dongle
messages.content_html: gui working on uconsole + UD100 dongle

messages.author: recrof
messages.text: here is how 1. flash raspbian 2. sudo git clone https://github.com/recrof/nrf_dfu_py/ /opt/nrf_dfu_py 3. copy dfu_service.sh to /opt/nrf_dfu_py , sudo chmod +x dfu_service.sh 4. copy nrf_dfu.service to /etc/systemd/system/ 5. rename newest RAK5631 firmware to firmware.zip and copy it to /boot/firmware/ - this is the same fat32 drive BOOTFS when you put microsd into windows or mac 6. sudo systemctl enable nrf_dfu 7. sudo systemctl start nrf_dfu 8. ... 9. PROFIT (edited)
messages.content_html: here is how 1. flash raspbian 2. sudo git clone https://github.com/recrof/nrf_dfu_py/ /opt/nrf_dfu_py 3. copy dfu_service.sh to /opt/nrf_dfu_py, sudo chmod +x dfu_service.sh 4. copy nrf_dfu.service to /etc/systemd/system/ 5. rename newest RAK5631 firmware tofirmware.zip and copy it to /boot/firmware/ - this is the same fat32 drive BOOTFS when you put microsd into windows or mac 6. sudo systemctl enable nrf_dfu 7. sudo systemctl start nrf_dfu 8. ... 9. PROFIT (edited)

messages.author: lucidnx
messages.text: For those who want to make their own flying radio noise generator: Battery, dimensions max.36x79x6mm: https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005009615451077.html or https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005008046901546.html RPI Zero W / 2 W compatible Charging module (pick one with coil, it's different one and it do have boost converter to 5Vout from battery): https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005008149660820.html Switch SS12F15 (I had some laying around, I believe it's these): https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005009988745326.html 1 toothpick to lock battery compartment with middle 3D printed part Red + black wires I will upload 3D models later WIth 1.2Ah battery I have, it can run hours. It's kinda overkill for what it's doing but Don't use battery smaller than 1Ah as it will be charged too fast. Almost all modules sold nowadays charge with 1A or more.. p.s.: if somebody know good charging module with buck converter for 5V output and programable charging current, let me know. or lithium battery that's around 600mAh and can be charged with 1A... (edited)
messages.content_html:
For those who want to make their own flying radio noise generator:
  • Battery, dimensions max.36x79x6mm:
https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005009615451077.html or https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005008046901546.html
  • RPI Zero W / 2 W compatible
  • Charging module (pick one with coil, it's different one and it do have boost converter to 5Vout from battery): https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005008149660820.html
  • Switch SS12F15 (I had some laying around, I believe it's these): https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005009988745326.html
  • 1 toothpick to lock battery compartment with middle 3D printed part
  • Red + black wires
I will upload 3D models later WIth 1.2Ah battery I have, it can run hours. It's kinda overkill for what it's doing but Don't use battery smaller than 1Ah as it will be charged too fast. Almost all modules sold nowadays charge with 1A or more.. p.s.: if somebody know good charging module with buck converter for 5V output and programable charging current, let me know. or lithium battery that's around 600mAh and can be charged with 1A... (edited)
